text_snippet: 2024 Engineering Salary Benchmark Report Software Engineer Salaries in the United States — by role, level, and city. Overview Software engineers remain among the highest paid roles in the tech industry. Although salaries have dropped by 9% to 15% in some segments due to mass layoffs, hiring freezes, the acceptance of remote work, and the increasing use of AI to automate engineering tasks, the long-term trend is positive. Salaries by U.S. Metro Area (Mid-level) Geographic differences are significant — the gap between the most expensive and least expensive U.S. cities has shrunk by two-thirds between 2019 and 2021, but a clear premium remains for established tech hu

AI and Machine Learning Compensation AI-related job postings increased by 15.7% in the past six months as of January 2024, while software engineer and developer listings decreased by 2.9%. OpenAI publishes typical compensation for a level-five engineer with 10+ years of experience of approximately $925,000 USD per year — a $300,000 base salary plus $625,000 in stock-based compensation. Some higher-level engineers earn as much as $1,400,000. The lowest reported salary at OpenAI was $210,000 for a

Insights from the Stack Overflow Developer Survey 2024: Technology Trends, Skill Demand, and Salary Patterns Survey of 65,000+ professional developers across 314 technologies. Salary data normalised to USD per year. 1. Most-Used Technologies Among professional developers, JavaScript remains the most used language (63.6%), followed by HTML/CSS (52.8%), Python (51.0%), SQL (49.3%), and TypeScript (38.5%). Java is reported by 30.3% of respondents, Bash/Shell by 27.2%, and C# by 27.1%. Go, Rust, Kot

$70,000 to $85,000. 4. AI Tooling Adoption Most developers (76%) use AI tools as part of their workflow. ChatGPT is the most popular at 74% adoption, followed by GitHub Copilot. Among developers currently using AI tools, 41% report wanting to use GitHub Copilot in the next year. Other tools like Tabnine, Cursor, and Codeium have smaller but growing user bases. Misinformation in AI results is the top ethical concern for 79% of developers, followed by source attribution at 65%. 5. Work Mode and Co

6. Case Study: Ruby Developer Challenges An analysis of 498,719 Ruby-related questions from Stack Overflow (2008-2024) combined with a survey of 154 Ruby developers identified 35 distinct topic areas grouped into six major categories.
